title: WAGDIE App User and System Flows

sourceOfTruth.note: Docs summarize current source; code and configuration remain authoritative. Route/API claims are grounded in app/, lib/, contexts/, hooks/, game/, and types/ files reviewed for this artifact.

participants.label: Visitor
participants.description: Unauthenticated reader exploring public pages, lore, map, videos, and character data.

participants.label: Wallet user
participants.description: Connected wallet holder who can sign in with Ethereum and use wallet-aware features.

participants.label: Token owner
participants.description: Wallet user who owns WAGDIE characters or concord assets and can perform owner-gated actions.

participants.label: Admin
participants.description: Authorized operator with access to admin review, canonization, map location, and searing-map tools.

participants.label: Next.js app router
participants.description: App Router pages in app/ provide public and admin entry points.

participants.label: Auth context
participants.description: Client-side auth state and SIWE session hydration in contexts/AuthContext.tsx.

participants.label: Transaction context
participants.description: Client-side transaction status/toast state in contexts/TransactionContext.tsx.

participants.label: Next API routes
participants.description: Route handlers under app/api that validate requests, enforce auth, and delegate to services.

participants.label: Service layer
participants.description: lib/services and lib/domain modules implementing business rules, validation, and composition.

participants.label: Supabase database
participants.description: Persistence layer reached through repositories/services for users, characters, lore, locations, and materialized state.

participants.label: Ethereum contracts
participants.description: WAGDIE, searing, spread, corpse, and concord contract ABIs used by wallet and sync flows.

participants.label: Phaser map runtime
participants.description: Game runtime bridged to React through game/PhaserGame.tsx, game/EventBus.ts, and map scene code.

participants.label: Eliza service
participants.description: AI chat, conversation, character, and location-room integration boundary exposed through app/api/eliza/*.

flows.title: Home and feature navigation
flows.summary: The landing page introduces the project and routes visitors into the core experiences: characters, map, searing, spread, videos, lore, and supporting content.
dataFlow: Static page content and card definitions present the major feature routes.
Navigation links hand off to App Router pages for each feature area.
Global layout/providers wrap pages with wallet, auth, query, and shared UI context where needed.

happyPath.action: Arrives at / and scans project overview cards.
Chooses a feature card or navigation item.
Renders the selected feature route such as /characters, /map, /searing, /spread, /videos, or /lore.

edgeCases.behavior: App Router renders the shared not-found boundary.
The app-level error boundary presents a controlled error state.

flows.title: Wallet connect and SIWE authentication
flows.summary: Wallet-aware features use Sign-In with Ethereum to create, hydrate, and clear a server-backed session while AuthContext exposes client auth state.
dataFlow: Client requests a nonce and stores the nonce cookie.
Wallet signs a SIWE message; verify route validates nonce/signature and upserts the user.
Session cookie is read by /api/auth/me and helper functions for protected APIs.
Logout clears the session cookie and AuthContext resets client state.

happyPath.action: Connects wallet and starts sign-in.
Requests /api/auth/nonce and prepares a SIWE message.
Signs the SIWE message with the connected wallet.
Verifies signature and nonce, creates/updates user, and sets a session cookie.
Hydrates current user from /api/auth/me for owner/admin-aware UI.

edgeCases.behavior: Verification fails and no authenticated session is created.
Auth helpers return an auth error for the route to translate into an HTTP error.
Logout route clears session state and client auth resets.

flows.title: Character browsing and filtering
flows.summary: Visitors browse the WAGDIE character collection with search/filter state, trait endpoints, and cards linking into per-token detail pages.
dataFlow: URL/search state and filter controls drive character list query parameters.
Character APIs return list data and filter facets.
Cards link to /characters/[tokenId] for detail, edit, chat, staking, and searing-adjacent experiences.

happyPath.action: Opens /characters and sees the token feed.
Filters by origin, alignment, trait, or text search.
Returns matching character data and facet values.
Selects a character card to view the detail page.

edgeCases.behavior: Browse UI shows an empty/no-characters state instead of failing.
Client hooks surface loading/error states while preserving the page shell.

flows.title: Character detail, ownership, and editing
flows.summary: A token detail page combines metadata, lore, ownership-aware edit controls, transaction history, searing status, and chat entry points.
dataFlow: Route tokenId drives character detail fetches and derived metadata display.
Auth/session and ownership checks determine whether edit actions are enabled.
PATCH requests use character permission helpers before updating editable character fields.
Related endpoints provide events, staking status, concord/searing information, and chat context.

happyPath.action: Opens a token detail page from browse, map, lore, or a direct URL.
Loads character metadata and related status endpoints.
Identifies whether the active wallet can edit the character.
Edits allowed sheet fields and saves changes.

edgeCases.behavior: Route/API validation prevents loading or updating an invalid character.
Edit controls are hidden or disabled and PATCH requests are rejected.
Character image display hook selects fallback behavior.

flows.title: Eliza character chat and conversations
flows.summary: Character chat and conversation UI routes messages through authenticated Eliza API endpoints while preserving conversation history.
dataFlow: Eliza auth validates wallet/session context separately from normal app auth where required.
Conversation hooks create or load conversation records.
Chat messages POST to /api/eliza/chat and receive assistant responses from the Eliza integration boundary.
Conversation list/detail APIs persist titles, messages, and deletion/archive operations.

happyPath.action: Opens chat from a character-aware UI.
Verifies Eliza auth and conversation permissions.
Sends a message in an existing or new conversation.
Returns an assistant response that is displayed and stored with conversation context.

edgeCases.behavior: Eliza routes reject the request before calling chat services.
Conversation route returns an error instead of leaking data.
Chat API reports failure and the client can keep the input context for retry.

flows.title: Lore archive and effective canon view
flows.summary: Lore pages render canon, community, character, event, location, and tweet-linked material through effective query helpers that combine base data and canonization overlays.
dataFlow: Page routes ask lore query helpers for archive or detail records by filter/slug.
Effective-query logic merges base lore data with community/canonization state.
Components render timelines, details, attribution, entity chips, media, and canon status.
Tweet API supports filtered tweet archive data where the lore UI needs external-source context.

happyPath.action: Opens /lore or a lore detail route.
Builds the effective lore dataset for archive filters or a slug lookup.
Renders archive/detail components with canonical/community status and source attribution.

edgeCases.behavior: Detail page calls notFound rather than rendering an empty detail shell.
Canonization helpers determine the effective view used by public pages.

flows.title: Community lore submission and revision
flows.summary: Token owners submit and revise community lore, with ownership checks, content validation, and lifecycle status managed by lore submission services.
dataFlow: Form captures Markdown/content, graph metadata, references, and submitter intent.
Public submission routes require an authenticated wallet and validate token ownership where applicable.
Validation normalizes content, metadata, and references before service-layer persistence.
Submitters can inspect and revise eligible submissions from their submission list/detail pages.

happyPath.action: Signs in and opens the lore submission form.
Adds lore content, metadata, references, and associated entities.
Validates auth, ownership, payload shape, and content constraints.
Creates or revises the submission with lifecycle status for admin review.

edgeCases.behavior: Submission APIs reject the request before validation/persistence.

edgeCases.condition: Submitter does not own referenced token
edgeCases.behavior: Ownership helper blocks owner-gated submissions.
Validation returns structured errors for the form to display.

flows.title: Admin lore submission review and publication
flows.summary: Admins review submitted lore, curate metadata, mark items in review, publish/unpublish community records, and promote/demote canon status.
dataFlow: Admin UI fetches queue/detail records through admin-only routes.
requireAdmin enforces operator permissions before action handlers execute.
Action routes share request parsing/error handling and delegate lifecycle transitions to loreSubmissionService.
Publication/canonization changes feed the public effective lore view.

happyPath.action: Opens the admin queue and filters submissions by status.
Reviews detail, sanitized preview, references, and graph metadata.
Validates admin session and invokes review/publish/canonize action route.
Applies lifecycle transition and publication/canon state updates.

edgeCases.behavior: Admin APIs return an auth error before exposing queue/detail data.
Transition helper rejects the operation and returns a validation error.
Reference validation blocks publication until corrected.

flows.title: Admin lore canonization overrides
flows.summary: Admins manage drafted and published canonization overrides that affect how public lore resolves its effective canon/community state.
dataFlow: Admin page lists canonization records and draft states.
Admin APIs require admin auth and delegate to loreCanonizationService.
Draft/save/publish/delete operations update override records.
Public lore effective-query helpers read canonization state when assembling the lore archive.

happyPath.action: Opens canonization admin and selects an event/record.
Edits draft override state and saves it.
Publishes the draft override.
Public lore views reflect the updated canonization result.

edgeCases.behavior: Admin route maps not-found service errors to HTTP errors.
Service validation rejects the draft/update.

flows.title: Interactive map exploration
flows.summary: The map page combines React HUD/sidebar state, Phaser rendering, marker/event bridges, and location APIs to let users explore WAGDIE geography.
dataFlow: Map page loads map data, event markers, locations, and selected character/location state.
React passes map props into PhaserGame and listens to EventBus events from MapScene.
Layer controls and HUD update visible markers and selected entities.
Location APIs/services provide editable map metadata shared with admin location tools.

happyPath.action: Opens /map and waits for asset/data loading.
Returns locations and map data used to build markers.
Renders the map scene and emits selection/hover events to React.
Toggles layers, selects markers, and opens contextual panels.

edgeCases.behavior: Map asset error/loading components keep the page from crashing.
Map UI renders no-data states and keeps base navigation usable.
Map README documents the React↔Phaser event boundary so implementation can be traced.

flows.title: Map staking panel and staking sync
flows.summary: Wallet users inspect location occupancy and character staking state from the map sidebar, perform staking-adjacent actions, and sync contract-derived state.
dataFlow: Sidebar selection identifies a location and candidate characters.
Staking-status APIs provide current persisted/materialized staking state.
Wallet/contract hooks perform blockchain transactions where enabled.
Post-transaction sync routes update server-side state so the map and character pages reflect the latest chain data.

happyPath.action: Selects a map location and opens the staking sidebar.
Loads staking status for relevant tokens/locations.
Initiates a staking/unstaking action through wallet-aware hooks.
Tracks transaction state and triggers post-transaction sync.

edgeCases.behavior: Wallet-aware controls prompt connection or remain disabled.
Transaction context/hook state surfaces failure without mutating local success state.
Sync endpoints refresh persisted state from chain-derived data.

flows.title: Location room and Eliza map interactions
flows.summary: Map locations can expose Eliza-backed room state, including location room reads and tick/sync operations for AI activity at a place.
dataFlow: Selected map location drives location room API calls.
Location-room route returns current room state and participants/messages as defined by location room types.
Tick/sync routes advance or reconcile Eliza-backed room state.
Panel renders room status inside the map staking/sidebar context.

happyPath.action: Selects a map location that supports a location room.
Fetches location-room state for the selected location.
Provides room state or advances it through a tick operation.
Views current room activity in the map sidebar panel.

edgeCases.behavior: Panel can render an unavailable/empty state instead of assuming room data.
Route auth blocks the request according to Eliza boundary rules.
Dedicated sync endpoint can reconcile location-room state.

flows.title: Searing and concord-driven previews
flows.summary: The searing experience uses concord ownership, concord-to-layer mappings, character metadata, preview composition, and sync routes to drive searing visual state.
dataFlow: Wallet/concord APIs identify concord assets owned or associated with a character.
Concord searing map domain resolves concord IDs to visual layer instructions.
Preview route composes character metadata and searing layers into a preview response.
Searing contract interactions and sync routes update persisted searing state.

happyPath.action: Opens /searing and connects wallet if needed.
Loads owned concords and character searing status.
Resolves searing layers and renders a preview for the selected character/concord combination.
Confirms searing action and syncs resulting state.

edgeCases.behavior: Searing UI can show an unavailable state and avoid preview/action calls.
Resolver/preview validation rejects invalid searing layer combinations.
Dedicated sync route can refresh per-character searing state.

flows.title: Spread, infection, cure, and corpse burning
flows.summary: The spread page orchestrates infection/spread interactions, cure actions, and corpse burning modals backed by wallet/contract hooks and character event state.
dataFlow: Spread page and components gather candidate character/action state.
Modal flows confirm infection, cure, searing-adjacent, or corpse burning actions.
Wallet hooks submit contract transactions and TransactionContext reports status.
Character event/sync APIs expose recent outcomes and refresh server-side materialization.

happyPath.action: Opens /spread and chooses an available action.
Reviews and confirms action in the relevant modal.
Processes spread/cure/corpse transaction through contract hooks.
Surfaces status and allows sync/event views to refresh.

edgeCases.condition: Action unavailable for selected token state
edgeCases.behavior: UI disables or redirects the user to a valid action path.
Transaction hook reports rejection and leaves action state unchanged.
Event API returns an error and the page can still show primary action UI.

flows.title: Admin map location editing
flows.summary: Admins create, edit, and delete map location metadata consumed by the public map and location-room experiences.
dataFlow: Admin page loads existing location records through location APIs.
Location form edits are validated and normalized by domain metadata helpers.
Location service persists create/update/delete operations.
Public map reads the same location data for marker/panel rendering.

happyPath.action: Opens /admin/map-locations and passes admin gate.
Creates or edits a location in the form.
Validates request and calls location service.
Normalizes metadata and persists the location.

edgeCases.behavior: Domain validation prevents saving malformed location metadata.
Delete confirmation component gates destructive action.
Admin UI/API protection blocks location edits.

flows.title: Admin concord-to-searing map editing
flows.summary: Admins maintain the mapping from concord IDs to searing layer behavior used by previews and searing composition.
dataFlow: Admin editor lists concord searing mappings.
CRUD APIs validate concord IDs and layer payloads.
Concord searing map service persists mappings.
Searing preview and resolver logic consume mappings for user-facing searing flows.

happyPath.action: Opens the searing-map editor.
Creates or updates a concord mapping.
Validates and persists mapping through service/domain helpers.
Searing flow uses the mapping for preview/layer resolution.

edgeCases.condition: Invalid concord ID or duplicate mapping
edgeCases.behavior: Input/domain validation rejects bad records.

edgeCases.condition: Mapping deleted while preview is requested
edgeCases.behavior: Searing resolver/preview handles missing mapping as unavailable/invalid.

flows.title: Background and operational sync endpoints
flows.summary: Dedicated sync routes reconcile ownership, staking, searing, and Eliza location-room materialized state from chain/external systems into app-readable data.
dataFlow: Sync endpoints are invoked after transactions, by operational jobs, or by admin/operator tooling.
Routes validate request shape and call subsystem-specific sync/materialization services.
Services read chain/external state and update database-backed app state.
Public pages and APIs consume the refreshed materialized state.

happyPath.action: Receives a sync request for ownership, staking, searing, or location rooms.
Queries chain or external integration state and normalizes results.
Stores refreshed materialized state for fast app reads.
Sees current state in character, map, searing, spread, or Eliza room UI.

edgeCases.behavior: Sync route returns an error and callers can retry without changing UI contracts.
Subsystem routes scope updates to their domain to avoid corrupting unrelated state.

relationships.label: SIWE session enables owner/admin editing and protected character APIs.
Lore submission create/revise requires an authenticated wallet and ownership checks.
Published/canonized submissions affect public lore archive and detail pages.
Canonization overrides shape effective canon/community status.
Map marker and sidebar selections can lead to character detail pages.
Staking actions rely on sync endpoints to refresh materialized state.
Both use Eliza route auth and integration boundaries for AI-backed interactions.
Concord-to-layer mappings maintained by admins drive searing previews.
Spread/cure/corpse actions intersect with character state shown in searing-related experiences.
Home navigation routes visitors into the core feature flows.
